name: Ingest API callbacks with Google Drive 24/7

parameters.text: =✅ Ingest API callbacks with Google Drive 24/7 — run completed at {{ $now.toFormat('HH:mm') }}

parameters.content:
### Receive Inbound Webhook

Entry point: listens for POST calls on `/wf09364-payload`. Use the TEST url while building, switch callers to the PRODUCTION url after publishing.

Next: Compute HMAC Signature

### Compute HMAC Signature

Computes the HMAC-SHA256 of the raw payload to verify the sender's signature before trusting the data.

Next: Signature Valid?

### Signature Valid?

Two-way gate on `={{ $json.computedSignature === $json.headers['x-signature']`. TRUE continues the happy path, FALSE takes the alternate branch.

Ports: ①→ Map to Canonical Schema · ②→ Reject (HTTP 401)

### Reject (HTTP 401)

Sends the HTTP 401 response back to the caller — keep this fast; heavy work happens after/async.

### Map to Canonical Schema

Shapes the item: writes `itemId`, `kind`, `payload`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Acknowledge Fast (HTTP 202)

### Acknowledge Fast (HTTP 202)

Sends the HTTP 202 response back to the caller — keep this fast; heavy work happens after/async.

Next: Route by Event Kind

### Route by Event Kind

Routes each item to one of 3 lanes: created, updated + fallback.

Ports: ①→ Handle 'created' → Google Sheets · ②→ Handle 'updated' → Forward API · ③→ Log Unrecognized Kind

### Handle 'created' → Google Sheets

Upserts a row into 'Payload Tracker', matched on `itemId` — re-running with the same key updates the row instead of duplicating it.

Ports: ①→ Consolidate Handling · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Handle 'updated' → Forward API

POST https://api.example.com/v1/payloads — retries 3× with backoff on failure.

Ports: ①→ Consolidate Handling · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 API key / bearer for the target API

### Log Unrecognized Kind

Appends a new row to 'Payload Tracker' — a human-readable audit trail that doesn't require opening n8n to inspect.

Ports: ①→ Consolidate Handling · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Consolidate Handling

Rejoins 3 branches into one stream (mode: append) so downstream logic runs once.

Next: Enrich with Reference Data

### Enrich with Reference Data

GET https://enrichment.example.com/v2/lookup — retries 3× with backoff on failure.

Ports: ①→ Fold In Enrichment Fields · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 API key / bearer for the target API

### Fold In Enrichment Fields

Shapes the item: writes `enrichedAt`, `enrichmentSource`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Serialize Run Output (CSV)

### Serialize Run Output (CSV)

Serializes items to a CSV binary file.

Next: Archive Copy to Google Drive

### Archive Copy to Google Drive

Uploads the generated file to Google Drive as a timestamped archive copy.

Ports: ①→ Load Previous Snapshot · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2

nodes.name: Note: Archive Copy to Google Drive

parameters.content:
### Load Previous Snapshot

Reads every row from 'Payload Tracker' — the read side of the store, e.g. for a digest or a downstream sync.

Ports: ①→ Compute Drift vs Snapshot · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Compute Drift vs Snapshot

Custom JavaScript: Compare current metrics with the previous snapshot

Next: Summarize Run Stats

### Summarize Run Stats

Shapes the item: writes `runAt`, `itemCount`, `status`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Append Audit Row (Google Sheets)

### Append Audit Row (Google Sheets)

Appends a new row to 'Payload Tracker' — a human-readable audit trail that doesn't require opening n8n to inspect.

Ports: ①→ Send Summary to Slack · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Send Summary to Slack

Posts a message to Slack (`#ops`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Capture Failure Context

Shapes the item: writes `failedNode`, `errorMessage`, `workflow`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Escalate Failure to Slack

### Escalate Failure to Slack

Posts a message to Slack (`#ops`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

Next: Park in Dead-Letter Queue

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Park in Dead-Letter Queue

Appends a new row to 'Payload Tracker' — a human-readable audit trail that doesn't require opening n8n to inspect.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

# Ingest API callbacks with Google Drive 24/7

**Pattern:** Webhook Pipeline · **Level:** Production · **Trigger:** Webhook

## What it does
Ingest API callbacks with Google Drive 24/7. It fires the moment an external system calls its webhook.

## Why it exists
Manual webhooks is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Receive Inbound Webhook → Compute HMAC Signature → Signature Valid? → Map to Canonical Schema → Acknowledge Fast (HTTP 202) → Route by Event Kind → Handle 'created' → Google Sheets → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to webhook pipeline without manual effort
- Anyone automating webhooks in software development
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Standardizes the process every time
- Runs 24/7 in the background
- Removes copy-paste errors

## Before first run
Connect credentials: API key / bearer for the target API; Google OAuth2; Google OAuth2 / service account; Slack OAuth2.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## Try it now
Sample data is pinned on the trigger — hit **Execute Workflow** to watch it flow through every node before connecting any real credentials. Unpin the trigger (right-click → Unpin) when you're ready to go live.

## Design decisions

The optional HMAC check runs before any business logic touches the payload — bad signatures are rejected at the door rather than after work has already been done with untrusted data. Acking fast (HTTP 202) and doing the real work after the response is what stops the sender's webhook delivery from timing out and re-delivering the same event.

## Practice exercises

Already production-grade — deepen your understanding:

1. Turn on signature verification and trace what a tampered payload does at the 'Signature Valid?' gate.
2. Add a new event kind to the switch and give it its own handler.
3. Remove the fast-ack step and observe how response time changes under a slow downstream write.

## 🛟 Error handling & retries

External calls retry **3× with 2s backoff**. Anything still failing exits through its node's error output into the red error rail → context is captured and the on-call channel is alerted. Failures are also parked in a dead-letter queue for replay. Set an n8n **Error Workflow** in settings as the final safety net.

## 🔐 Security

Never trust inbound payloads: the webhook path is unguessable, and the HMAC signature is verified before any processing. Add header auth or IP allow-listing for defense in depth. Secrets live in n8n credentials — never in node parameters.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your webhooks process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
